content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcnr- PRACTTcEs C orrarrarssroN
428 J Street o Suire 620 r Sacramento, CA 95gll-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
February 10,2009
Mary Lou Mongar
Manager, CandidatelPublic Services
San Bernardino Registrar of Voters
777 E. Rialto Ave
San Bernardino, CA 92415-0770
Rel Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-09-023
Dear Ms. Mongar:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of San Bernardino County for
advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act";.t
auESTroN
May committees involved in the Chino Valley Independent Fire District election
to be held on August 25,2009, combine the semi-annual statement with the second pre-
election statement due on July 16, 2009?
CONCLUSION
In connection with an August 25,2009 election, committees may use a filing
schedule that combines the semi-annual campaign statement with the second pre-election
statement due on July 16, 2009.
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Secrions 8 1000 through 91014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections I8l I0 through l8gg7 of Tiile 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulabry references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Califbrnia Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
"E?h'


File No. A-09-023
Page No. 2
During an election, candidates and committees involved in the election are
required to file two campaign statements disclosing contributions received and
expenditures made in connection with that electionl Th"r" pre-election.u-poign
statements are generaU-ri]ea 40 days before the election and again rz aoy, [erore the
election' (Section 84200.8.) Semi-annual statements are filed January 3l and July 31,
respectively. (Section 84200.)
There is a mechanism in the Act that permits the combining of statements when
an overlapping reporting period occurs, or th; filing of the semi-annual statement would
be an additional, unnecessary filing to the two pre-iection statements. (Section g4205.)
In this case, filing the two pre-eleciion statements in connection with *'eujurt zs,2oog,
election will satisfy the semi-annual filing requirement.
A campaign statement filing schedule which outlines the due dates for filing
statements in connection with the election is enclosed for your convenience.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me
at (916) 322-5660.
ANALYSIS
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Barbara Smith
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
BGS jgl
Enclosure
